meta.probe: Likert 1-7 restricted softmax, English only, anti-neutrality framing

personas.indonesia: You are a person from Indonesia.
personas.usa: You are a person from the United States.
personas.china: You are a person from China.

meta.note: These are persona-based priors, all measured in English. Persona and generation language are independent — these numbers describe value priors by cultural identity, not by output language.

items.statement: Traditional cultural practices should be preserved even when they conflict with modern values.
items.notes: Strongest item for the ID-US headline cell. Cultural ordering (ID > CN > US) is coherent and stable across all runs. US is 4-collapsed (P(digit=4)=0.961) — US starts at effective neutrality. ID-US divergence is the primary signal. Best Phase 1 debut item.

items.statement: Individual freedom is more important than social harmony.
items.notes: Highest delta in the set. Clean digit distributions for all three personas (no 4-collapse). However, the ID-US pair starts nearly identical (gap=0.007) — this item cannot support an ID-US debate. Use for CN-involving pairs only. CN persona clearly more collectivist than ID/US in English.

items.statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
items.notes: Most marginal item (passes by 0.001). Anti-neutrality framing broke US 4-collapse here (US modal at digit 3, P=0.765). CN ordering is counterintuitive — CN persona (0.361) is slightly more pro-individual than US (0.372), inverting the collectivism expectation. US-CN gap is negligible (0.012) — this item cannot support a US-CN debate. ID vs US and ID vs CN divergence is genuine.
